text.title: mob-programming Core Rules
text.summary: Eight testable rules for mob sessions.

rule.statement: For an idea to go from your head to the computer, it must go through someone else's hands. The Driver types only what the Navigator dictates.
rule.rationale: Strong style forces verbalisation, which catches mistakes and spreads knowledge.

rule.statement: Navigate at the intent level, not the dictation level. "Create a User class with name+email" beats "type public class User".
rule.rationale: Intent-level navigation teaches; dictation-level wastes everyone's time.

rule.statement: Rotate the driver every 5-10 minutes, enforced by a visible timer. No exceptions during the learning phase.
rule.rationale: Without timer + rotation, mob degenerates into pair + spectators.

rule.statement: Write a single-sentence Done definition before the session. Display it visibly throughout. If you cannot write it, do not start.
rule.rationale: No Done = the session drifts; visible Done = anchor for time-boxed disagreements.

rule.statement: Take a real break (away from the screen) every 75-90 minutes.
rule.rationale: Skipping breaks drives introvert attrition and post-mob burnout.

rule.statement: Hold a written retrospective after every session: what worked, what slowed us, one experiment for next time.
rule.rationale: Without retro, mistakes repeat; written retro creates a learning trail.

rule.statement: Time-box disagreements to 2 minutes, then try one approach. Experiments beat debates.
rule.rationale: Code is cheap to change; consensus debates are expensive.

rule.statement: Maintain a parking lot for off-topic items. Review at the retrospective.
rule.rationale: Off-topic items derail flow; parking preserves them without interrupting.

rule.statement: Done defined, rotation timer running, parking lot open, retrospective scheduled.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion for the positive branch.

rule.statement: Group too small or too large; task is independent parallelisable work; no Done definable.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion for the negative branch.
